title: Control Action Event Semantics
purpose: Formalize the control plane event families before any control plane integration is implemented. Every control plane action must emit a governed, replay-addressable event. Intent and execution must be separated: the control plane emits intents; an orchestration layer resolves them.
architectural_constraints: Every action requires approval, execution, and outcome events
Operator attribution must be preserved in every action event
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines all 8 event families: control.action.requested, control.action.approved, control.action.rejected, control.action.executed, control.action.failed, control.action.rolled_back, control.suggestion.generated, control.recipe.invoked, control.guard.blocked
Each event family specifies required fields: action_id, actor, timestamp, intent, outcome, causality_chain
Approval chain semantics documented: requested → approved/rejected → executed/failed → rolled_back (optional)
Policy defines which actions require human approval vs auto-approval
